question: Why is the project intentionally simple?
title: Why is the project intentionally simple?
summary: One operator, SQLite, and text-only ideas keep deployment and risk small.

scenes.headline: The absences are intentional.
scenes.body: No multi-tenant product, no application logins, and no file uploads. Those boundaries protect a single-operator design—not unfinished placeholders waiting to be filled in silently.

cards.title: One person, not a team
cards.teaser: The app assumes one trusted operator.
points: The app assumes one trusted operator.
There are currently no application user accounts.
Adding a login page alone would not make it safely multi-user.
Multi-user support would require ownership fields and ownership validation in every relevant query.

cards.title: SQLite, not a database server
cards.teaser: A file database keeps deploy and backups simple.
points: SQLite keeps deployment and backups simple.
Database file and directory permissions matter.
Concurrent multi-user writes are not part of the current design.

cards.title: Text, not uploads
points: File uploads would introduce storage, validation, permission, malware, quota, backup, and deletion concerns.
Uploads should be treated as a new subsystem, not a small form enhancement.
